title: Multiply and divide by powers of 10
description: Practice multiplying and dividing by powers of 10.
topic_path: Multiply and divide by powers of 10

hints:
###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit **one place to the left**.  


Drag the dot all the way to the right.


[[☃ interaction 2]]
So, if we multiply by ${10}$ $\greenD{\text{two}}$ times, we move every digit **$\greenD{\text{two}}$ places to the left.**

Let's look at how the pattern continues.  

 | | | | 
-: | -: | :-: | -: | :-: | -:
$32.689\times\blueD{10^1} = $ |$32.689\times\blueD{10}$ | $=$ | $326.89$
$32.689\times\greenD{10^2} = $ |$32.689\times\greenD{10\times10}$ | $=$ | $3268.9\phantom{0}$
$32.689\times\purpleC{10^3} = $ |$32.689\times\purpleC{10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $32{,}689\phantom{.00}$

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&32.689 \times 10^1 =326.89\\\\
&32.689 \times 10^2 =3268.9\\\\
&32.689 \times 10^3 =32{,}689
\end{align}$

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the left.  

Drag the  dot all the way to the right.


 [[☃ interaction 2]]
So, if we multiply by ${10}$ $\greenD{\text{two}}$ times, we move every digit **$\greenD{\text{two}}$ places to the left.**

Let's look at how the pattern continues.  

 | | | |
-: | -: | :-: | -: | :-: | -:
$0.258\times\blueD{10^1} = $ |$0.258\times\blueD{10}$ | $=$ | $2.58$
$0.258\times\greenD{10^2} = $ |$0.258\times\greenD{10\times10}$ | $=$ | $25.8\phantom{0}$
$0.258\times\purpleC{10^3} = $ |$0.258\times\purpleC{10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $258\phantom{.00}$
$0.258\times\maroonD{10^4} = $ |$0.258\times\maroonD{10\times10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $2580\phantom{.00}$

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&0.258 \times 10^2 =25.8\\\\
&0.258 \times 10^3 =258\\\\
&0.258 \times 10^4 = 2{,}580
\end{align}$

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the left.  

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


 [[☃ interaction 7]]  

*Notice we add a zero in the empty place value.*
So, if we multiply by ${10}$ $\purpleC{\text{three}}$ times, we move every digit **$\purpleC{\text{three}}$ places to the left.**

Let's look at how the pattern continues.  

 | | | | 
-: | -: | :-: | -: | :-: | -:
$95\times\blueD{10^1} = $ |$95\times\blueD{10}$ | $=$ | $95\blueD{0}$
$95\times\greenD{10^2} = $ |$95\times\greenD{10\times10}$ | $=$ | $9{,}5\greenD{00}$
$95\times\purpleC{10^3} = $ |$95\times\purpleC{10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $95{,}\purpleC{000}$
$95\times\maroonD{10^4} = $ |$95\times\maroonD{10\times10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $95\maroonD{0{,}000}$
$95\times\goldD{10^5} = $ |$95\times\goldD{10\times10\times10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $9{,}5\goldD{00{,}000}$

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&95\times 10^3 = 95{,}000\\\\
&95\times 10^4 =950{,}000\\\\ 
&95\times 10^5 = 9{,}500{,}000
\end{align}$

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the left.   

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


 [[☃ interaction 1]]
So, if we multiply by ${10}$ $\purpleC{\text{three}}$ times, we move every digit **$\purpleC{\text{three}}$ places to the left.**

Let's look at how the pattern continues.  

 | | | | 
-: | -: | :-: | -: | :-: | -:
$29.1\times\blueD{10^1} = $ |$29.1\times\blueD{10}$ | $=$ | $291$
$29.1\times\greenD{10^2} = $ |$29.1\times\greenD{10\times10}$ | $=$ | $2{,}910$
$29.1\times\purpleC{10^3} = $ |$29.1\times\purpleC{10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $29{,}100$
$29.1\times\maroonD{10^4} = $ |$29.1\times\maroonD{10\times10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $291{,}000$
$29.1\times\goldD{10^5} = $ |$29.1\times\goldD{10\times10\times10\times10\times10}$ | $=$ | $2{,}910{,}000$

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&29.1 \times 10^3 =29{,}100\\\\
&29.1 \times 10^4 =291{,}000\\\\
&29.1 \times 10^5 =2{,}910{,}000
\end{align}$

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the right.  

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


 [[☃ interaction 1]]
So, if we divide by ${10}$ $\greenD{\text{two}}$ times, we move every digit **$\greenD{\text{two}}$ places to the right.**

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&28{,}770\div 10^2 =287.7\\\\
&28{,}770\div 10^3 =28.77\\\\
&28{,}770\div 10^4 =2.877
\end{align}$

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the right.  

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


 [[☃ interaction 1]]

$\begin{align}
&64{,}000\div 10^3 =64\\\\

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the right.  

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


 [[☃ interaction 1]]
So, if we divide by ${10}$ $\greenD{\text{two}}$ times, we move every digit **$\greenD{\text{two}}$ places to the right.**

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&72{,}910\div 10^2 =729.1\\\\
&72{,}910\div 10^3 =72.91\\\\

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

$\begin{align} 9.1\times &\blueD{10^2} \\\\
9.1\times  &\blueD{10\times10} \\\\
9.1\div &\blueD{10^2} \\\\
9.1\div  &\blueD{10\div10} 
 \end{align}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the left.  

So, multiplying by ${10}$ $\blueD{\text{two}}$ times, moves every digit **$\blueD{\text{two}}$ places to the left.**

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


[[☃ interaction 2]]



*Another way to think about this is that the decimal moves two places to the right.*

Dividing by $10$ moves every digit one place to the right.

So, dividing by ${10}$ $\blueD{\text{two}}$ times, moves every digit **$\blueD{\text{two}}$ places to the right.**

Drag the dot all the way to the right.


[[☃ interaction 2]]

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&9.1 \times 10^2=910 \\\\
&9.1 \div 10^2=0.091
\end{align}$

[[☃ interaction 3]]

Here are the completed equations:

$\begin{align}
&1{,}615 \times 10^2 = 161{,}500\\\\
&1{,}615 \div 10^2= 16.15
\end{align}$

$\begin{align} 3.7\times &\blueD{10^1} \\\\

 \end{align}$

[[☃ interaction 2]]

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations with powers of $10$.**

$411.92 \times {}$[[☃ expression 4]] $=411{,}920$

$411.92 \times {}$[[☃ expression 5]] $=41{,}192$

$411.92 \times {}$[[☃ expression 1]] $=4{,}119.2$
hints:
###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the left.

Multiplying by a power of $10^\heartsuit$ moves every digit $\heartsuit$ places to the left.

Number | Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$411.92$ |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ || $4$ | $1$ | $1$ | $.$ | $9$ | $2$
$411{,}920$ | $4$ | $1$ | $1$ | $,$ | $9$ | $2$ | $0$ |||

The digits in $411.92$ moved $3$ places to the left. So we multiplied $411.92$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
411.92 \blueD{\times10 \times 10 \times 10} &=411{,}920 \\\\
411.92 \blueD{\times10^3} &= 411{,}920
\end{align}$

Number | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
 :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$411.92$ |$\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ || $4$ | $1$ | $1$ | $.$ | $9$ | $2$
$41{,}192$ | $4$ | $1$ | $,$ | $1$ | $9$ | $2$ | ||

The digits in $411.92$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $411.92$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
411.92 \maroonD{\times10 \times 10} &=41{,}192 \\\\
411.92 \maroonD{\times10^2} &= 41{,}192
\end{align}$

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
 :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$411.92$ |$\purpleC{\leftarrow}$ || $4$ | $1$ | $1$ | $.$ | $9$ | $2$
$4{,}119.2$ | $4$ | $,$ | $1$ | $1$ | $9$ | $.$ | $2$ | 

The digits in $411.92$ moved $1$ place to the left. So we multiplied $411.92$ by $1$ factor of $10$.

$\begin{align}
411.92 \purpleC{\times10} &=4{,}119.2 \\\\
411.92 \purpleC{\times10^1} &= 4{,}119.2
\end{align}$

Here are the completed equations.

$\begin{align}
411.92 \blueD{\times10^3} &= 411{,}920\\\\
411.92 \maroonD{\times10^2} &= 41{,}192 \\\\
411.92 \purpleC{\times10^1} &= 4{,}119.2
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations with powers of $10$.**

$63.85 \times {}$[[☃ expression 8]] $=6{,}385$

$63.85 \times {}$[[☃ expression 9]] $=63{,}850$

$63.85 \times {}$[[☃ expression 10]] $=638{,}500$
hints:
###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Multiplying by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the left.

Multiplying by a power of $10^\heartsuit$ moves every digit $\heartsuit$ places to the left.

Number | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
 :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$63.85$ |$\purpleC{\leftarrow}$ || $\purpleC{\leftarrow}$ | $6$ | $3$ | $.$ | $8$ | $5$
$6{,}385$ | $6$ | $,$ | $3$ | $8$ | $5$ |  |  | 

The digits in $63.85$ moved $2$ places to the left. So we multiplied $63.85$ by $2$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
63.85 \purpleC{\times10\times10} &=6{,}385 \\\\
63.85 \purpleC{\times10^2} &= 6{,}385
\end{align}$

Number | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
 :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$63.85$ |$\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ || $\maroonD{\leftarrow}$ | $6$ | $3$ | $.$ | $8$ | $5$
$63{,}850$ | $6$ | $3$ | $,$ | $8$ | $5$ | $0$ | ||

The digits in $63.85$ moved $3$ places to the left. So we multiplied $63.85$ by $3$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
63.85 \maroonD{\times10 \times 10\times 10} &=63{,}850 \\\\
63.85 \maroonD{\times10^3} &= 63{,}850
\end{align}$

Number | Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$63.85$ |$\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ || $\blueD{\leftarrow}$ | $6$ | $3$ | $.$ | $8$ | $5$
$638{,}500$ | $6$ | $3$ | $8$ | $,$ | $5$ | $0$ | $0$ |||

The digits in $63.85$ moved $4$ places to the left. So we multiplied $63.85$ by $4$ factors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
63.85 \blueD{\times10 \times 10 \times 10\times 10} &=638{,}500 \\\\
63.85\blueD{\times10^4} &= 638{,}500
\end{align}$

Here are the completed equations.

$\begin{align}
63.85 \purpleC{\times10^2} &= 6{,}385\\\\
63.85 \maroonD{\times10^3} &= 63{,}850 \\\\
63.85 \blueD{\times10^4} &= 638{,}500
\end{align}$

$720{,}800 \div {}$[[☃ expression 9]] $=7.208$

$720{,}800 \div {}$[[☃ expression 11]] $=720.8$

###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the right.

Dividing by a power of $10^\heartsuit$ moves every digit $\heartsuit$ places to the right.

Number | Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths | Thousandths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$720{,}800$ | $7$ | $2$ | $0$ | $,$ | $8$ | $0$ | $0$ ||||
$7.208 $ |$\blueD{\rightarrow}$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ || $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ | $7$ | $.$ | $2$ | $0$| $8$

The digits in $720{,}800$ moved $5$ places to the right. So we divided $720{,}800$ by $5$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
720{,}800  \blueD{\div 10 \div 10 \div 10\div 10\div 10} &=7.208 \\\\
720{,}800 \blueD{\div 10^5} &= 7.208
\end{align}$

Number | Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths 
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$720{,}800$ | $7$ | $2$ | $0$ | $,$ | $8$ | $0$ | $0$ |||
$72.08 $ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ || $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | $7$ | $2$ | $.$ | $0$| $8$

The digits in $720{,}800$ moved $4$ places to the right. So we divided $720{,}800$ by $4$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
720{,}800  \maroonD{\div 10 \div 10\div 10\div 10} &=72.08 \\\\
720{,}800 \maroonD{\div 10^4} &= 72.08
\end{align}$

Number | Hundred thousands | Ten thousands | Thousands | $,$ | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths 
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$720{,}800$ | $7$ | $2$ | $0$ | $,$ | $8$ | $0$ | $0$ ||
$720.8 $ | $\purpleC{\rightarrow}$ | $\purpleC{\rightarrow}$ | $\purpleC{\rightarrow}$ || $7$ | $2$ | $0$| $.$ | $8$

The digits in $720{,}800$ moved $3$ places to the right. So we divided $720{,}800$ by $3$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
720{,}800  \purpleC{\div 10\div 10\div 10} &=720.8 \\\\
720{,}800 \purpleC{\div 10^3} &= 720.8
\end{align}$

Here are the completed equations.

$\begin{align}
720{,}800 \div \blueD{10^5}&=7.208\\\\
720{,}800 \div \maroonD{10^4}&=72.08\\\\
720{,}800 \div \purpleC{10^3}&=720.8
\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the equations with powers of $10$.**

$624 \div {}$[[☃ expression 9]] $=62.4$

$624 \div {}$[[☃ expression 10]] $=6.24$

$624 \div {}$[[☃ expression 11]] $=0.624$
hints:
###What is a power?

A power is a shortcut way of writing repeated multiplication.

$\redD{\text{base}}\rightarrow\redD{a}^{\purpleD{b}\leftarrow\purpleD{\text{exponent}}}$

We mean "$\purpleD{b}$ factors of $\red{a}$." 

For example:

$\redD{10}^\purpleD{3}=\underbrace{\redD{10}\times \redD{10}\times \redD{10}}_{\purpleD{3\text{ factors}}}$

Dividing by ${10}$ moves every digit one place to the right.

Dividing by a power of $10^\heartsuit$ moves every digit $\heartsuit$ places to the right.

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | 
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | 
$624$ | $6$ | $2$ | $4$ | |
$62.4 $ | $\blueD{\rightarrow}$ |$6$ | $2$ | $.$ | $4$

The digits in $624$ moved $1$ place to the right. So we divided $624$ by $1$ divisor of $10$.

$\begin{align}
624  \blueD{\div 10} &=62.4 \\\\
624 \blueD{\div 10^1} &= 62.4
\end{align}$

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths 
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$624$ | $6$ | $2$ | $4$ | ||
$6.24 $ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ | $\maroonD{\rightarrow}$ |$6$ | $.$ | $2$ | $4$

The digits in $624$ moved $2$ places to the right. So we divided $624$ by $2$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
624  \maroonD{\div 10\div 10} &=6.24 \\\\
624 \maroonD{\div 10^2} &= 6.24
\end{align}$

Number | Hundreds | Tens | Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths | Thousandths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$624$ | $6$ | $2$ | $4$ | ||| 
$0.624 $ | $\purpleC{\rightarrow}$ | $\purpleC{\rightarrow}$ | $\purpleC{\rightarrow}$ |$.$ | $6$ | $2$ | $4$

The digits in $624$ moved $3$ places to the right. So we divided $624$ by $3$ divisors of $10$.

$\begin{align}
624  \purpleC{\div 10\div 10\div 10} &=0.624 \\\\
624 \purpleC{\div 10^3} &= 0.624
\end{align}$

Here are the completed equations.

$\begin{align}
624 \div \blueD{10^1}&=62.4\\\\
624 \div \maroonD{10^2}&=6.24\\\\
624 \div \purpleC{10^3}&=0.624
\end{align}$
